question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The official data shows CPI increased only 0.3% in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution. Since the actual result is already known and was 0.3%, the probability of the market resolving YES is 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing March 2026 CPI increased 0.3%
Market resolution requires CPI increase greater than 0.9%
Actual result (0.3%) is well below threshold (0.9%)
Market has not yet resolved despite data being available
risks: Data revision risk - CPI data can be revised but unlikely to change by 0.6+ percentage points
Market resolution timing - market may take time to settle despite clear outcome
